claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 9 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 9 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 9 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions.

Let's start with the basics: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. His name is Alex, and I'm wondering if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I'm happy to help you determine if you can claim Child Benefit for Alex.

To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions. Let's start with:

**How old is Alex?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you! That's helpful. Since Alex is 9 years old, he's under 16, which is good for Child Benefit purposes.

Next question: **Does Alex live with you in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me here in the UK. We're in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you for confirming that. 

Next, I need to ask: **Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?** (Remember, only one person can get Child Benefit for a child.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him. It's just me looking after him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment.

**Do you or your partner (if you have one) have an individual income over £60,000 per year?** 

(This won't affect whether you're eligible, but it may mean you need to pay a High Income Child Benefit tax charge.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of us earns that much. My income is well below £60,000.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me just confirm one final detail:

**Are there any special circumstances I should know about, such as: Is Alex in hospital or care for an extended period, or does he live with someone else for part of the time?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nothing like that. Alex just lives at home with me full-time. No special circumstances at all.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:
- Alex is 9 years old (under 16) ✓
- He lives with you in the UK ✓
- You are responsible for him ✓
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him ✓
- There are no special circumstances affecting eligibility ✓

You meet all the requirements to claim Child Benefit for Alex. Additionally, since your income is below £60,000, you won't need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge.

You can proceed with making a claim for Child Benefit. If you haven't already done so, you'll need to make a new claim to start receiving the benefit.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 9 years old, which is under 16, meeting the age requirement for Child Benefit. He lives with the claimant in the UK (Manchester), which satisfies the residence requirement. The claimant is responsible for Alex as they live together. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, which is necessary as only one person can claim for each child. There are no special circumstances such as Alex being in hospital, care, or living with someone else. The claimant meets all the eligibility criteria for claiming Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 9-year-old son Alex. All eligibility criteria are met: Alex is under 16, lives with the claimant in the UK, the claimant is responsible for him, and no one else is claiming for him. The claimant's income is below £60,000, so they would not face the High Income Child Benefit tax charge. There are no special circumstances that would affect eligibility.

No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, which is necessary as only one person can claim for each child. There are no special circumstances such as Alex being in hospital, care, or living with someone else. The claimant meets all the eligibility criteria for claiming Child Benefit for Alex.
